dataIdInfo.idPurp: Converts ArcGIS feature layers into interactive Leaflet web maps with optional embedded GeoJSON or external data loading.
dataIdInfo.idAbs:
This toolbox converts ArcGIS feature layers (vector point, line, and polygon data) into interactive Leaflet-based web maps through a two-step workflow.

The first tool, Symbology to Attributes, accepts an feature layer as input and extracts supported primary symbology types, specifically Single Symbol, Unique Values, and Graduated Colors. All other primary symbology types are not supported. In addition, value expressions are not supported for any symbology type, including the supported ones.

Symbology is derived using ArcPy and layer CIM, including default colors for out-of-range values. The tool creates a derived copy of the source feature class associated with the input feature layer and enriches it with additional attribute fields containing the extracted symbology. It also supports configurable color encoding: by default, Leaflet mode uses 6-digit HEX with a separate opacity field, while a custom option allows 8-digit HEX or CSS RGBA formats.

The second tool, Attributes to Leaflet, accepts the enriched feature class as input and generates an interactive Leaflet web map. It supports configurable tooltips, basemap selection (active map background color or predefined Esri/CARTO web tile layers), and two output modes: self-contained HTML with embedded GeoJSON or external GeoJSON loading.

The resulting output is a browser-ready Leaflet web map for interactive visualization.

searchKeys.keyword: Leaflet; Web Mapping; ArcGIS Pro; Feature Layer; Symbology Conversion; GeoJSON; HTML Export; Interactive Maps; Web GIS; Python Toolbox; Cartography
